---
title: "Neural Audio Theory"
subtitle: "Engineering foundations for AI music generation"
author:
  - name: "Eduardo J. Barrios"
    email: "edujbarrios@users.noreply.github.com"
date: "2026-06-29"
format:
  edujbarrios-revealjs:
    slide-number: c/t
    show-slide-number: all
    transition: fade
    background-transition: fade
    code-copy: true
---

## Neural Audio Theory

::: {.eyebrow}
Product presentation example
:::

**Neural Audio Theory** is an open-source documentation product for people who
want to understand how modern AI music systems are built, from signal
processing to transformers, diffusion models, prompt conditioning, and
evaluation.

[neural-audio-theory.vercel.app](https://neural-audio-theory.vercel.app/) ·
[github.com/edujbarrios/neural-audio-theory](https://github.com/edujbarrios/neural-audio-theory)

::: {.tag-list}
::: {.tag}
AI Music
:::
::: {.tag}
Open Source
:::
::: {.tag}
Documentation
:::
::: {.tag}
Engineering Education
:::
:::

## The Problem

::: {.two-column}
::: {.brand-card}
### AI music is accessible

Creators can generate full tracks with prompt-based tools, but the underlying
systems often feel like black boxes.
:::

::: {.brand-card}
### The theory is scattered

Audio DSP, embeddings, training losses, model architecture, ethics, and creator
workflows usually live in separate resources.
:::
:::

## The Product

::: {.eyebrow}
Learn how the machine hears
:::

Neural Audio Theory packages the field as a structured learning website:

- **Engineering-focused documentation** for FFTs, embedding geometry, losses,
  and generation pipelines
- **Architecture guides** for transformers, diffusion models, VAEs, GANs, and
  U-Nets in audio
- **Creator-facing workflows** for prompting, production, troubleshooting,
  mixing, stems, and vocals
- **Responsible-use material** covering copyright, training data, and ethical
  deployment

## Audience

::: {.three-column}
::: {.metric}
<span class="value">01</span>
<span class="label">developers</span>
:::

::: {.metric}
<span class="value">02</span>
<span class="label">researchers</span>
:::

::: {.metric}
<span class="value">03</span>
<span class="label">technical creators</span>
:::
:::

::: {.brand-card}
The repository is intentionally aimed at developers, researchers, and
technically-minded contributors. The live website is the learning surface; the
repo is the source, configuration, and content architecture behind it.
:::

## Learning Journey

::: {.two-column}
::: {.compact-card}
### Beginner to practical

Introduction, beginner explanations, Suno prompting, ethics, legal context,
producer workflows, tools, APIs, and AI music agents.
:::

::: {.compact-card}
### Technical to research-level

Audio fundamentals, representations, embeddings, model zoo, mathematics,
architecture, training, evaluation, and advanced multimodal generation.
:::
:::

## Feature Map

::: {.three-column}
::: {.brand-card}
### Docs

Docusaurus-powered content with a 16-section sidebar from intro to glossary.
:::

::: {.brand-card}
### Math

KaTeX rendering for signal-processing and model-training explanations.
:::

::: {.brand-card}
### Interactivity

React components for latent space visualization and prompt constructor
utilities.
:::
:::

## Technology

| Layer | Choice |
|---|---|
| Documentation | Docusaurus 3.10 |
| UI | React 19 |
| Language | TypeScript 6 |
| Math | remark-math, rehype-katex, KaTeX |
| Hosting target | Static site on Vercel |
| License | MIT |

## Developer Workflow

```bash
git clone https://github.com/edujbarrios/neural-audio-theory.git
cd neural-audio-theory
npm install
npm start
npm run typecheck
npm run build
```

## Positioning

::: {.section-title}
## Sure, you drop AI tracks. Now learn how they actually work.
:::

## Why It Matters

::: {.two-column}
::: {.brand-card}
### For builders

Move from prompt experimentation to system understanding: representations,
conditioning, orchestration, evaluation, and deployment tradeoffs.
:::

::: {.brand-card}
### For creators

Translate creative goals into better technical intuition around prompts, stems,
mixes, vocals, and generated audio behavior.
:::
:::

## Open Source Invitation

::: {.eyebrow}
Contribute the next page
:::

The project is built as an open-source educational documentation site. Good
contributions can be new pages, clearer explanations, interactive components,
bug reports, or improved references.

::: {.tag-list}
::: {.tag}
MIT License
:::
::: {.tag}
Docusaurus
:::
::: {.tag}
TypeScript
:::
::: {.tag}
AI Audio
:::
:::
